data.title: Allbirds, Hims & Hers, Microsoft, Xanadu Quantum And Tesla: Why These 5 Stocks Are On Investors' Radars Today
data.description:
Major U.S. indices closed mixed on Wednesday, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average slipping 0.15% to 48,463.72, while the S&P 500 rose 0.8% to 7,022.95 and the Nasdaq jumped 1.6% to 24,016.01.

These are the top stocks that gained the attention of retail traders and investors through the day:

Allbirds Inc. (NASDAQ:BIRD)

Allbirds saw a remarkable increase of 582.33%, closing at $16.99. The stock reached an intraday high of $24.31 and a low of $6.11, with a 52-week range between $24.31 and $2.15. In the after-hours trading, the stock fell 24.66% to $12.80.

The company’s pivot from sneakers to AI, branded as “NewBird AI,” has captivated investors. This transition involves selling its footwear assets for $39 million and investing in AI infrastructure with a $50 million financing facility. 

Hims & Hers Health Inc. (NYSE:HIMS)

Hims & Hers Health stock experienced a 13.72% rise, closing at $24.29. The stock fluctuated between $24.70 and $21.28 during the day, with a 52-week high of $70.43 and a low of $13.74. In the after-hours trading, the stock rose 10.25% to $26.78.

Analyst Allen Lutz maintained a Neutral rating but reduced the price target to $21, citing potential margin pressures from a shift to branded GLP-1s and growing international revenue, according to a recent report.

Microsoft Corp. (NASDAQ:MSFT)

Microsoft shares climbed 4.63%, closing at $411.22. The stock’s intraday high was $414.37, with a low of $396.73, and it has a 52-week range of $555.45 to $355.67. In extended trading, the stock rose 1.16% to $415.98.

The tech giant’s shares rose amid a Nasdaq-led risk-on session, bolstered by a competitive AI-software landscape and new add-ins for Word and Teams.

Xanadu Quantum Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ:XNDU)

Xanadu Quantum Technologies Inc. surged 69.79%, closing at $25.18. The stock hit a high of $28.25 and a low of $16.15, with a 52-week high of $28.25 and a low of $6.97. The stock rose 8.42% to $27.30 in the after-hours session.

Xanadu Quantum Technologies shares surged after Nvidia unveiled "Ising," the first open-source AI model family designed for quantum computing, sparking a broader sector rally. Nvidia said the models improve error correction with 3x higher accuracy and up to 2.5x faster decoding than current standards.

Tesla Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA)

Tesla stock rose by 7.63%, closing at $391.95. The stock’s high was $394.65, with a low of $362.50, and it has a 52-week range of $498.83 to $222.79.

Analyst Itay Michaeli maintained a bullish outlook, adjusting the price target to $490. The stock benefited from a strong Consumer Discretionary sector, which was the top-performing sector today. 

Benzinga Edge Stock Rankings show AllBirds stock doesn’t check out on Short, Medium And Long Price Trends.

This story was generated using Benzinga Neuro and edited by Shivdeep Dhaliwal

data.title: Joe Lonsdale Was Once Rejected For A PayPal Internship After An 'Argument' With Max Levchin — Here's How The Palantir Co-Founder Bounced Back
data.description:
Joe Lonsdale, co-founder of Palantir and managing partner at 8VC, once recalled that his early attempt to join PayPal began with a rejection from Max Levchin.

Early Rejection At PayPal Interview

Lonsdale recalled that while studying at Stanford University, he noticed many of the "smartest, most interesting people" around him heading to PayPal, which at the time had only around 70 to 80 employees.

He decided to apply, but his first interview did not go well.

"He had me on the whiteboard and we argued about something. I didn't get an offer," Lonsdale said, referring to his initial interview with PayPal co-founder Levchin.

He added that although he was turned down, the experience stuck with him as PayPal continued attracting top engineering talent.

Second Chance Leads To PayPal Internship

Despite the setback, Lonsdale was later invited back after meeting PayPal leadership again, including Peter Thiel. This time, he was offered a position and joined the company as an intern while still in college.

"They actually rejected me my freshman year, but they let me in my sophomore year," he said in a 2026 conversation on the PBD Podcast.

Inside The Early PayPal Era

Lonsdale said PayPal was still a small but rapidly growing company during his time there, expanding from under 100 employees to a few hundred.

The company's founding team included Thiel, Levchin, Elon Musk and others who would later build or fund major tech firms.

It was one company started by Thiel and a bunch of his smartest friends, he said, adding that the environment made it a magnet for ambitious engineers.

From Rejection To Billionaire Investor

Today, Lonsdale is a billionaire investor with a net worth of $2.9 billion, according to Forbes.

His company, Palantir, which he co-founded with Thiel, currently has a net worth of $339.83 billion.

Meanwhile, 8VC, which manages about $6 billion in assets, has backed a number of major startups in the defense technology and life sciences sectors.

Photo Courtesy: Elle Aon on Shutterstock.com

data.title: If You Invested $1000 In Synopsys Stock 20 Years Ago, You Would Have This Much Today
data.description:
Synopsys (NASDAQ:SNPS) has outperformed the market over the past 20 years by 7.42% on an annualized basis producing an average annual return of 16.16%. Currently, Synopsys has a market capitalization of $84.25 billion.

Buying $1000 In SNPS: If an investor had bought $1000 of SNPS stock 20 years ago, it would be worth $19,947.68 today based on a price of $439.78 for SNPS at the time of writing.

Finally -- what's the point of all this? The key insight to take from this article is to note how much of a difference compounded returns can make in your cash growth over a period of time.
